contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 30
- Description: Capoeira practice fix — one music track per move, no repeats
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/capoeira/pull/31
  
  Details:
  Root cause: pickMusic in session-generator.js was hard-coded to return 4 tracks regardless of how many moves the session had. Sessions are 5-7 moves (ginga + 4-6 theme moves), so practice-flow.js's playback indexing — music[index % music.length] — wrapped 2-3 moves back to earlier tracks. Result: every session had audible repeats.
  
  Fix: pickMusic defaults count to selectedMoves.length and returns one track per move; existing used-Set dedup logic was already correct, it just wasn't being asked for enough.
  
  Generalized the tempo arc via new buildTempoArc(n):
    n=4 -> Slow,Medium,Medium,Slow (existing shape preserved)
    n=5 -> Slow,Medium,Medium,Medium,Slow
    n=6 -> Slow,Medium,Medium,Fast,Medium,Slow (single Fast peak in middle)
    n=7 -> Slow,Medium,Medium,Fast,Medium,Medium,Slow
    n=8+ -> same shape, sustained Medium section expands
  
  Slow opener pairs with ginga warm-up at index 0; Slow cool-down at index n-1. From n>=6 onward there's room for a Fast peak at the middle-most position to give the session an energy spike.
  
  Future-proofing: if a future move count exceeds the music library (today: 11 tracks, max 7-move sessions, so unreachable), pickMusic falls back to a random repeat for the overflow — repeat as lesser evil than silence.
  
  Verified across 80 simulated sessions (20 trials each for move counts 5,6,7,8): zero repeats in any session.
  
  practice-flow.js modulo (music[index % music.length]) left in place as defensive code — now a no-op in the normal path but graceful if a future caller passes a short music array.
  
  PR: TrueSightDAO/capoeira#31 (squash-merged 25e912c).
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 50.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
